articles.title: This was one of the most vulnerable moments ever for a US government

children.content: The ABC Americas editor was inside the ballroom in Washington DC when the drama unfolded as a shooting suspect was stopped by the Secret Service.

image.altText: ABC’s John Lyons inside the White House correspondents' dinner

children.content: US Acting Attorney-General Todd Blanche says the shooter apprehended at the White House correspondents' dinner was likely targeting Donald Trump. Follow live.

items.title: Trump likely target of shooting at correspondents' dinner, top US official says

updates.title: White House: 'This political violence has to end'
Buckingham Palace confirms king's visit to US will go ahead
Trump says shooting suspect is a 'sick guy'

items.title: Trump was being shown a party trick. Then gunshots rang out

image.altText: a composite image of a female security service agent with gun in front of satellite image of Washington DC

children.content: Thousands of influential players in Washington filed into a ballroom with the US president. Then shots rang out.

items.title: Trump says shooter's 'manifesto' shows he is a 'sick guy'

children.content: Several US media outlets are reporting on the existence of the manifesto, which reportedly states the shooter wanted to target administration officials and was sent to the suspect's family members just before the attack.

image.altText: US President Donald Trump posted a picture of the suspected shooter on social media.

items.title: Trump was 'all set to really rip', but shooting has changed tune on media

children.content: While the latest act of violence wasn't as close as Trump's brush with death in Butler, Pennsylvania, almost two years ago, it will no doubt revive some trauma for Trump and those closest to him.

image.altText: Trump in a tuxedo

items.title: 'Membership to nothing': The multi-million-dollar Wild West of rewards clubs

children.content: "Rewards clubs" have exploded in Australia, claiming to have given away hundreds of millions in prizes, but they operate with almost no legal scrutiny.

gallery.altText: A large 4 wheel drive in a showroom clouded in smoke with dramatic red lighting.

thumbnail.altText: A large 4 wheel drive in a showroom clouded in smoke with dramatic red lighting.

items.title: Iran daily briefing: Israel sets sights deeper into Lebanon despite ceasefire

children.content: It's a ceasefire in name only between Israel and its targets in Lebanon, as the wait continues for more talks between the US and Iran.

image.altText: Man with Jerusalem in the background

items.title: Israel demands residents evacuate villages in southern Lebanon

children.content: Israeli forces have demanded residents in seven villages north of Lebanon's Litani River to evacuate.

image.altText: Several trees surrounding an army vehicle driving through the dirt.

items.title: Treasury's impossible task: Prepare a budget as oil shock unfolds

children.content: Accompanying the oil shock unfolding in the Strait of Hormuz is a profound change in the way fiscal and monetary policymakers operate. And as the government prepares the budget, with the impact of the Iran war still unfolding, a recession may be ahead.

image.altText: A man in a suit from a side profile with his chin jutted out defiantly.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Trump is political kryptonite in Australia, but Albanese must walk the line

children.content: As Donald Trump's approval in Australia sinks, Anthony Albanese needs to walk a careful line: keep voters convinced of the merits of the alliance, while avoiding the impression he is being too reverential.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese takes a selfie with a smiling Donald Trump

items.title: China's 'quirky' fix for property crisis rewards marathon runners

children.content: Local governments in China are offering marathon runners, AI experts and tennis players subsidies to purchase unwanted properties amid a lingering real estate downturn.

gallery.altText: an unfinished residential development in China

thumbnail.altText: an unfinished residential development in China

items.title: Street artist Peter Drew on why his 'Aussie' posters are still important

children.content: Peter Drew has been hanging 'Aussie' posters in cities around Australia for 10 years, and has no plans to stop.

image.altText: A man with dark hair and a big smile

items.title: US allies turning to Japan for defence supplies as Iran War drags on

children.content: Australia's multi-billion-dollar deal with Japan for its future warships could set a template that will help Tokyo rapidly expand defence exports across Asia.

image.altText: A grey frigate is seen in the foreground, sailing in Sydney Harbour, with the bridge to the right of the picture.

items.title: Two-hour barrier smashed twice in history-making London Marathon

children.content: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe has become the first man to run a marathon in under two hours, winning the London Marathon with a time of 1:59:30.

image.altText: A runner in pink has his arms outstretched while crossing the finish line, world record time on a screen above his head.

items.title: More families are sleeping in cars. But they're not the highest housing priority

children.content: An analysis of 12 months of urgent housing and homelessness attendances in Tasmania has shown an alarming spike in people with dependent children needing help — while overcrowding is an increasing concern.

image.altText: A cartoon illustration of a woman and two children who are living in their car

items.title: Why women are getting less sleep than men — and what we can do about it

children.content: There are biological and psychological reasons behind why women experience more disrupted sleep than men, but experts say the mental load is another powerful yet less obvious contributor.

gallery.altText: illustration of woman lying awake next to her sleeping partner

thumbnail.altText: illustration of woman lying awake next to her sleeping partner

items.title: Quiz: Have you got the memory to beat our history quiz?

children.content: Which Australian city played host to Expo 88? And what age did the world's oldest spider reach?

image.altText: Three Expo 88 hostesses smile for a photo

items.title: How friendship with Russell Crowe led to an MMA fight

children.content: Bosch & Rockit filmmaker Tyler Atkins's latest feature put his stars through the wringer on camera, but held them with love behind the scenes.

image.altText: A man in a blue shirt leans on a walking stick in front of a boxing ring

items.title: Australia to sell fleet of Bushmaster armoured vehicles to the Dutch

children.content: Australian Bushmaster armoured vehicles are being sent to the Netherlands following a request to Australia by the Dutch government.

thumbnail.altText: A Bushmaster sporting Ukrainian flags stands in front of a Globemaster airplane on the tarmac

items.title: Law breaking drivers to be targeted amid horror year on Queensland roads

children.content: The ABC last year revealed a sharp drop in road safety enforcement hours from police in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic.

image.altText: Police officers at checkpoint with driver line-up at Queensland-NSW border at Coolangatta.

items.title: Government to fund security upgrades for family violence victim-survivors

children.content: Almost 1,700 women facing the danger of family violence will be able to upgrade their home security under a $3.5 million pledge by the Victorian government.

image.altText: A fist in the foreground, a woman blurred in the background.

items.title: Sixteen dead in strikes as Ukraine marks 40 years since Chernobyl

children.content: Marking the anniversary, Volodymyr Zelenskyy warns that Russia is using "nuclear terrorism" as a weapon of war.

gallery.altText: Three people wearing hazmat suits holding vigil candles at a memorial.

thumbnail.altText: Three people wearing hazmat suits holding vigil candles at a memorial.

items.title: Platypus researcher says the species is just 'hanging on' in NSW

children.content: A leading researcher is calling for the platypus to be recognised as a threatened species in NSW, saying a lack of data is the only reason it has not been reclassified.

image.altText: Close up of a platypus face, poking out of a box.

items.title: Subdivisions taking place in Adelaide's 'visionary' Hills Face Zone

children.content: The Hills Face Zone was legislated to protect the natural character of Adelaide's landscape backdrop but, despite subdivisions not being permitted, there have been 11 over the past decade.

image.altText: Face of hillside stretching into distance with some houses among trees

items.title: Hani Watson learned the importance of listening to her rogue bones and 'Nigel'

children.content: When Hani Watson settles under the barbell at the Glasgow Commonwealth Games, she will be lifting more than just the plates on the bar.

gallery.altText: Weightlifter Hani Watson flexes her right arm while holding a bronze medal with the other.

thumbnail.altText: Weightlifter Hani Watson flexes her right arm while holding a bronze medal with the other.

items.title: Foord stars for Arsenal, Kerr equals record in WSL

children.content: Arsenal's Matildas are scenting more Champions League glory after the holders beat OL Lyonnes 2-1 in the first leg of their semi-final at the Emirates Stadium, while Matildas captain Sam Kerr scored twice to keep Chelsea's slim WSL title ambitions alive in a 4-1 win over Everton.

image.altText: Caitlin Foord celebrates with Olivia Smith

items.title: AI is helping rangers protect Victorian wildlife in a fraction of the time

children.content: A new AI tool will help Parks Victoria manage native and endangered species affected by natural disasters, including the summer bushfires, with the department making the technology available to everyone.

image.altText: A green square frames a small brown mouse-like animal that stands on hind legs beside a pole in grassland.

items.title: Veteran's advice after Anzac Day booing: Find your 'warrior spirit'

children.content: While condemnation has been swift after Indigenous elders were booed on Anzac Day, solutions to this type of racism seem harder to pin down. Elders have advice to share.

articles.title: Concerns community cohesion is 'going out the door' after Anzac booing

children.content: Jeers could be heard for more than a minute as Uncle Ray Minniecon spoke at yesterday's Anzac service in Sydney with similar scenes playing out in Melbourne and Perth.

image.altText: a group of young indigenous males march in redfern at the coloured diggers ceremony for anzac day 2026

articles.title: No Acknowledgement of Country at Anzac event attended by Qld premier

children.content: The event in Townsville, which has a proud military history and is Australia's largest garrison city, also did not include a Welcome to Country.

image.altText: David Crisafulli speaking at a lecturn.

gallery.altText: uncle ray at anzac day service at redfern

thumbnail.altText: uncle ray at anzac day service at redfern

items.title: Taylor avoids backing ASIO amid questions on ISIS-linked families

children.content: Government action needs to be taken to prevent a group of Australian families with links to former ISIS fighters from re-entering the country, Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says.

image.altText: A man in a blue jacket and brown pants sits on a chair gesticulating with one hand.

items.title: Dawson fights back tears after return as GWS edge Roos amid ARC drama

children.content: Adelaide skipper Jordan Dawson breaks down in tears after playing his first match since his brother's tragic death, as GWS prevails against North with the aid of a major ARC blunder.

image.altText: Jordan Dawson shares a heartfelt hug with Hugh McCluggage

items.title: Manly's revival continues as Foran sets record, Panthers down Knights

children.content: Manly's revival under interim coach Kieran Foran has continued with a fourth straight win, as the Sea Eagles scored five tries in the second half to sink the Parramatta Eels.

image.altText: Reuben Garrick of the Sea Eagles runs with the ball

items.title: Calls for better security at Sydney hospitals after 'alarming' alleged attack

children.content: A 51-year-old man seriously injured a nurse's leg at Prince of Wales Hospital in Randwick about 8:30pm on Saturday night, before assaulting two other nurses and a security guard.

image.altText: Prince of Wales Hospital 2025-01-27 11:01:00

items.title: Aussie wins first World Surf League title at Margaret River Pro

children.content: Australian surfer George Pittar defeats a quartet of Brazilian world champions to claim a maiden championship tour win at the Margaret River Pro.
